name: Pot Roast Under Pressure
recipeIngredient: 1 ½ cup beef stock or 1 can (14 oz) beef broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the meat well on all sides in a pot with a lid.
2. Add the beef stock or beef broth to cover the bottom of the pot by about an inch.
3. Slice the carrot, onion, and celery into similar-sized pieces (about ½ inch).
4. Place the lid on the pot, ensuring it is secure.
5. As steam begins to escape from the pressure release valve, add the pressure cooker weight to the pot.
6. Reduce heat to low/medium and allow the contents to gently bubble for about 1 hour under pressure.
7. Once done cooking, carefully release the steam using the quick-release method.
8. Discard any spent vegetables as they were only for flavor (you may give them to your dog).
9. Add fresh vegetables of your choice (carrots, onions, potatoes, celery, etc.) in desired quantities and allow them to cook in the broth until tender.
10. Remove the meat and vegetables from the pot and thicken the gravy if desired before serving. Enjoy!
